dimensions.name: coverage
dimensions.evidence: All 6 deliverables from definition.yaml have dedicated research documents: gate-check-hook-fix.md, transition-simplification.md, review-consent-isolation.md, enforcement-wiring.md, specialist-reasoning-upgrade.md, specialist-expansion.md. Synthesis document confirms all 6 with readiness assessment.

dimensions.name: evidence-basis
dimensions.evidence: Every research document includes a Sources Consulted table with tier classifications. Codebase claims cite specific paths and line numbers: bin/frw.d/hooks/gate-check.sh regex, bin/rws lines 1361-1560, common.sh:87 has_passing_gate(), skills/implement.md lines 25-33. Two tertiary sources (WAI-ARIA, Diataxis) cited for background frameworks, not codebase facts.

dimensions.name: specificity
dimensions.evidence: Most specific: gate-check-hook-fix quotes exact regex 's/.*rws +transition +([^ ]*).*/\1/p' and proposed fix regex, names common.sh:87 has_passing_gate(), traces exact failure path. Least specific: transition-simplification acknowledges 'What step-modifier language works? Research defers to implementation' as explicit deferral. All documents include file paths, line numbers, and function names.

dimensions.name: contradiction-check
dimensions.evidence: One potential tension: gate-check-hook-fix proposes fixing the hook while transition-simplification suggests hook may become redundant post-simplification. Explicitly reconciled in transition-simplification: 'Decide if gate-check hook is still needed post-simplification' and execution order puts hook fix first. No unacknowledged contradictions found.

dimensions.name: seed-sync
dimensions.evidence: seed_id: furrow-fed8, seed status: researching, row step: research. Expected mapping: research -> researching. Match confirmed. Seed is not closed (closed_at: null).

summary: All 5 dimensions pass — research covers all 6 deliverables with specific evidence, consistent findings, and synchronized seed status.
